MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Barbara R. Adams, Esq. of ADAMS, NYE, TRAPANI, BECHT (415-982-8955), appearing for defendant / moving party Amcord, Inc.
John D. Van Ackeren, Esq. of BERKES CRANE ROBINSON (213-955-1150), appearing for defendant / moving party CalPortland Company, (FKA as California Portland Portland Cement Company)
James P. Nevin, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Rany George

RULING:
Defendant Amcord Inc.'s motion for summary judgment was heard with Defendant CalPortland's motion for summary judgment. Argued and tentative ruling adopted as corrected: Defendant Amcord's motion is denied. Amcord failed to satisfy its burden of production on the issue of causation since plaintiff's discovery responses were not factually devoid. Plaintiff testified at his deposition that he worked with Riverside Gun Plastic Cement. Amcord's interrogatory responses state that Riverside Gun Plastic Cement contained asbestos during the period plaintiff contends that he was exposed to it. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Mary Ellen Scherer, CSR # 5208

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
John D. Van Ackeren, Esq. of BERKES CRANE ROBINSON (213-955-1150), appearing for defendant / moving party Calportland Company, (FKA as California Portland Portland Cement Company)
Barbara R. Adams, Esq. of ADAMS, NYE, TRAPANI, BECHT (415-982-8955), appearing for defendant / moving party Amcord, Inc.
James P. Nevin, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Rany George

RULING:
Defendant CalPortland Company (FORMERLY KNOWN AS CALIFORNIA PORTLAND CEMENT COMPANY)'s motion for summary judgment was heard with defendant AMCORD, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant CalPortland's motion is denied. There is a triable issue whether Mr. George's shortness of breath and other health problems at the time of his retirement were caused by exposure to asbestos. Mr George's declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos containing Colton Gun Plastic Cement while he was employed by Edward Sugrhue. CalPortland's D'Amico objections are overruled. The Court further ordered that plaintiff will submit to an in-person deposition. Said deposition is limited to two hours and the matters in his declaration. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Mary Ellen Scherer, CSR # 5208
